title: 'Kattar Beimaan Party': PM Modi attacks AAP at Jalandhar rally
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi criticised Punjab's ruling Aam Aadmi Party for corruption allegations. He labeled the party "Kattar Beimaan Party" during a rally in Jalandhar. The prime minister also highlighted the state's deteriorating law and order situation. He mentioned a former AAP leader's conviction for murder during Delhi riots. Modi urged voters to elect the BJP for a prosperous Punjab.

title: Stranger than fiction: Messi, Yamal set for WC final 19 years after first meeting
content: Lionel Messi and Lamine Yamal will face each other in the World Cup final.  Nineteen years ago, Messi bathed baby Yamal during a charity photoshoot event.  Yamal has since become Spain's biggest football star at Barcelona.  Messi left Barcelona in 2021, while Yamal emerged as a global superstar.  This remarkable full-circle moment culminates in Sunday's biggest football match.

title: Hormuz closure: Why crude oil prices haven’t spiralled out of control
content: Brent crude prices in the spot market briefly surged to around $144 per barrel before easing as markets adjusted. Yet as ADB notes in its report - despite this unprecedented supply shock, crude oil prices did not reach levels seen during the 1973 Arab oil embargo or even the 1990 Gulf war, if one were to inflation-adjust the prices.

title: US may add $100,000 bond to Green Card applicants seeking permanent residence
content: The US government is considering a $100,000 bond for Green Card applicants. This measure aims to ensure immigrants are financially self-sufficient and do not burden public programs. The Trump administration is exploring this long-standing legal authority under the Immigration and Nationality Act. This proposal follows earlier attempts to impose fees on H-1B visa applications. Officials stated this policy reduced overstays in pilot programs.

title: Spanish PM, King to attend World Cup final as Argentine President stays home
content: Spain's Prime Minister and King will attend the World Cup final match.  US President Donald Trump also confirmed his attendance at the New York stadium.  Argentina's President Javier Milei will watch from home due to superstition.  He believes his presence at home brings Argentina luck in matches.  This decision follows a tradition of avoiding presidential attendance at crucial games.

title: Winners will make the hard choices: Microsoft's security chief to employees after layoffs
content: Microsoft's new security chief Hayete Gallot has replaced senior executives, merged engineering teams, and cut hundreds of jobs to push the company toward AI-powered cybersecurity, The Information reports. In an internal memo, Gallot told staff the industry is being reimagined and rewards firms that make hard choices early. Microsoft is chasing AI security spending flowing to rivals OpenAI and Anthropic.

title: Mom spends $17K turning basement into hockey rink for her sons
content: Amanda Vernaci created a hockey rink in her basement over three months.  This ambitious DIY project cost approximately seventeen thousand dollars for her family.  She began her DIY journey in a rental home, sharing budget-friendly projects.  Her skills developed further while pregnant, teaching herself to use tools.  The basement rink was built around her sons' passion for the sport

title: Only 354 West African leopards remain, but one park offers hope
content: West African leopards are showing resilience in Benin's Pendjari National Park.  Conservation efforts are succeeding despite regional conflict and human pressures.  Leopard density has increased significantly between 2017 and 2023.  Rangers and scientists are implementing innovative protection and monitoring strategies.  This success offers hope for threatened species in unstable areas.

title: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman admits he scared millions of people around the world
content: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman acknowledges past challenges while anticipating a strong year ahead. He stated the company does not want AI to frighten people. Instead, AI should provide users with more freedom and control. Altman believes artificial intelligence will create new opportunities for many individuals. The team is working on new products and updates for users.

title: 'Go to your MLA': BJP legislator's response to water crisis sparks protest in MP's Shivpuri
content: In an incident highlighting political disconnect, a BJP MLA disregarded the pleas of villagers at a public grievance meeting, insisting they were not his voters. In protest, the villagers placed a child atop the MLA’s vehicle to draw attention to their pleas concerning water and electricity shortages. The striking act of dissent has taken social media by storm, with the protest video circulating widely among users.

title: I went to Israel nervous. I returned with more questions than answers
content: Beyond the war headlines lay coexistence, political argument, cultural mixing and contradictions that no short visit could fully resolve.

title: Why railways picked Jind-Sonipat route for its 1st hydrogen train
content: India has joined select nations operating hydrogen-powered trains with its first service. The Jind-Sonipat route was chosen for its low traffic and infrastructure availability. This 10-coach train is the longest of its kind globally. It utilizes indigenous technology and a powerful fuel-cell propulsion system. The service will eventually be extended to New Delhi.

title: Cocaine & guns: How FIFA's World Cup final referee was caught up in a police raid
content: Slavko Vincic will referee the FIFA World Cup 2026 final between Argentina and Spain.  This prestigious assignment follows a past controversy involving a police raid.  Vincic was questioned as a witness and cleared of any wrongdoing.  He has previously officiated a match involving Argentina in 2022.  His career has continued to flourish after the incident.

title: FIDE Awards shortlists: Gukesh, Praggnanandhaa, Divya lead India's strong showing
content: Indian chess stars Gukesh, Praggnanandhaa, and Divya Deshmukh are nominated for FIDE Excellence Awards.  They will compete against global talent in multiple prestigious player categories.  The All India Chess Federation also received a nomination for Best Federation.  Indian national teams and ChessBase India Foundation earned recognition for their achievements.  Winners will be announced at the Chess Olympiad.

title: Ram Mandir donation case: Tinnu Yadav, Manish Yadav sent to 39-hour police remand
content: Two individuals, Ram Shankar Yadav and Manish Yadav, received a 39-hour police remand on Friday. This remand is connected to the alleged embezzlement of Ayodhya Ram temple donations. Custodial interrogation is crucial for uncovering the conspiracy and tracing the money trail. Earlier, two other accused were also taken for custodial interrogation as part of the ongoing probe. The alleged donation scam has triggered significant political backlash against the ruling BJP.

title: India's U-19 WC-winning star arrested over alleged attempt to bribe player
content: Former India U-19 star Manjot Kalra was arrested in Sri Lanka on Friday.  He faces allegations of attempting to bribe a player during the Lanka Premier League.  Kalra, a co-owner of the Jaffna Kings, was taken into custody by investigators.  The alleged bribery attempt was reported by several Jaffna Kings players.  Sri Lanka Cricket stated it will cooperate with the ongoing investigation.

title: NRI says neighbor called cops when she and her husband were arguing
content: An Indian woman in Texas sought help on social media after her husband was taken by the police based on the complaint of a neighbor who also told the police that the husband frequently shouted at the wife. The wife said when police came they saw minor injury on her as she was pushed during the argument.

title: Vijay's 'Jana Nayagan' undergoes major revamp after online leak ahead of July release
content: Jana Nayagan's release is now set for July 23 after facing censorship and leak challenges.  The producer confirmed significant modifications and additions were made to the film.  These alterations occurred after some footage leaked online weeks before its censor certificate.  The film's title card for Vijay has also reportedly been updated by the makers.  Audiences will experience a very different version of the movie in theatres.

title: Mumbai mayor rejects BMC report on Chembur tree crash that killed 11-year-old
content: Mumbai Mayor Ritu Tawde rejected the BMC inquiry report on the Chembur tree crash.  She directed the civic administration to conduct a fresh investigation into the incident.  Corporators criticized the report for failing to fix accountability for the child's death.  The report imposed only a nominal penalty on the contractor, which was deemed insufficient.  Vihaan Srivastav's father also questioned the inquiry's transparency and accountability.

title: Beware of clicking on 3 dots: You may lose money to cons
content: Cyber experts warn about remote device takeover fraud scams targeting mobile users.  A Lucknow woman lost over three lakh rupees in such a sophisticated scam.  Fraudsters posed as customer support executives to trick the victim's child.  They obtained screen-sharing permissions by asking for prompts to be tapped.  This allowed criminals to access banking apps and steal funds.

title: 'To atone donation theft, seek forgiveness': Ayodhya Ram temple trust begins 10-day purification ritual
content: A 10-day purification ritual has begun in Ayodhya for alleged donation theft.  The Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust initiated this special ceremony.  This ritual involves daily recitations of the Vishnu Sahasranama Stotra.  The Valmiki Ramayan is also being recited as part of the purification.  This event follows weeks after the alleged theft triggered political controversy.

title: RITES: Double-decker flyover undercuts Bengaluru Metro gains
content: Engineering consultancy RITES has flagged issues with Bengaluru's proposed double-decker flyover. The report suggests this plan could undermine the Metro's purpose and encourage private vehicle use. Building the structure may also present significant engineering and operational challenges for the city. This proposal runs contrary to national policies promoting sustainable urban mobility. The Karnataka government will now decide the project's future course.

title: 'Harassed by seniors': Teacher drives car into Godavari river, kills self and 2 kids
content: A teacher and his two children died after their car plunged into the Godavari river. Police suspect the tragic incident was a deliberate act of suicide. The teacher had posted a WhatsApp status alleging harassment by his seniors. Local residents alerted authorities, and rescue teams recovered the vehicle and bodies. An investigation is now underway into the allegations and the deaths.

title: Ex-Badrinath temple officer arrested in donation theft case after SIT grilling
content: Former Badrinath temple officer Rajendra Chauhan was arrested for alleged donation theft. CCTV footage showed Chauhan collecting cash bundles and putting them into his pocket. The Special Investigation Team received an internal inquiry report from the temple committee. Investigators identified more suspects after examining fresh CCTV footage from recent dates. The investigation into the temple donation theft case is currently ongoing.

title: Why India's first hydrogen train is special - explained
content: With the launch of a hydrogen train, India has undoubtedly taken a big leap in the sphere of greener transportation.  India’s first hydrogen-powered train has 10 coaches, with the capacity to carry 2,600 passengers. It will run between Jind and Sonipat in Haryana.

title: Pakistan star suspended after failing dope test during T20 World Cup
content: Pakistan all-rounder Mohammad Nawaz received a three-month ban for a doping violation.  His suspension will reduce to one month upon completing a treatment program.  Nawaz tested positive for a cannabis metabolite during the T20 World Cup.  He admitted the offense, stating it was unrelated to sporting performance.  His match records from February 7 to May 1 have been disqualified.

title: Your landlord sold the property—can the new owner evict you?
content: When a property is sold in India, a tenant's rental agreement does not simply disappear. The new landlord is bound by the original terms set by the previous owner. Rent hikes can only occur if specified in an escalation clause within the contract. Tenants who continue paying their old landlord post-sale remain protected, and their rights to the security deposit are preserved regardless of the ownership change.

title: Affordable housing demand drives DDA to record Rs 1,020 crore Q1 FY27 sales
content: Delhi Development Authority generated record revenue of Rs 1,020 crores in the first quarter. Robust housing demand across all categories significantly fueled these impressive financial results. Sub city Narela emerged as the biggest contributor, selling 1,153 flats in the EWS category. Multiple housing schemes and infrastructure development contributed to the strong sales performance. DDA's efforts have led to record housing sales building upon previous financial years.

title: A Chhatrapati kicked it off. Messi and Ronaldo rule Kolhapur now
content: Kolhapur celebrates football with giant player cutouts during the World Cup.  Local clubs, established decades ago, foster a strong footballing tradition.  These clubs rely on public donations and community support for their operations.  The city has hosted international matches and draws large crowds to local games.  Kolhapur's devotion to football continues to grow despite infrastructure limitations.

title: Crops wait as monsoon plays truant in Telangana
content: Delayed monsoon rains have slowed Telangana's farm activity to its lowest levels. Paddy cultivation is the biggest casualty of the uneven rainfall pattern this season. Farmers are holding back from transplanting, waiting for sustained showers to begin. Sowing has covered only 45.4% of the normal cultivated area as of July 14. Telangana received 158.4 mm rainfall against the normal 228 mm, a deficit of 30%.

title: Why Argentina President is skipping the World Cup final despite Trump's presence
content: Argentine President Javier Milei will not attend the World Cup final in New Jersey. He believes his superstition, known as cábalas, ensures Argentina’s victory. Milei will watch the match from his residence in Buenos Aires. Donald Trump and FIFA president Gianni Infantino are expected to attend the final. This decision follows a long-standing presidential tradition of avoiding crucial matches.

title: Ex-cricketer backs Rohit to sign off from ODIs with 'innings that people remember'
content: Patel said the India opener has repeatedly shown during his career that he responds strongly after a lean run and expects him to do so again if Sunday's match turns out to be his last.

title: Mumbai-Ahmedabad bullet train update: Progress so far and Japan's E10 plan
content: India's first bullet train project between Mumbai and Ahmedabad is progressing well. Initial operations will commence using Indian high-speed trains in 2027. Japan's next-generation E10 Shinkansen trains will be introduced later, after 2030. The first operational stretch will connect Surat and Bilimora in Gujarat. This initiative significantly reduces travel time between the two major cities.

title: Bank loans have become accessible to all eligible people after 2014: Nirmala Sitharaman
content: PM Modi's vision helped bring a shift in banking culture ensuring easy credit flow to small businesses and freeing borrowers from clutches of private money lenders, says Union Finance Minister at a credit outreach programme in A.P.; ₹3,216 cr. is being distributed to over one lakh beneficiaries in Palnadu, says CM Chandrababu Naidu

title: Census self-enumeration in Tamil Nadu: CM Vijay, Governor Arlekar appeal for active public participation
content: Chief Minister Mr. Vijay officially completed his self-enumeration process. The Governor urged every household to extend full cooperation to the Census authorities.

title: Jantar Mantar protest LIVE: Sonam Wangchuk dehydrated; fasting student leaders advised not to walk
content: Delhi HC to hear PIL on July 20 over 'continuous, intrusive' police surveillance at Jantar Mantar protest

title: Mysuru Dasara expo grounds to get year-long amusement: Yathindra
content: Yathindra says the government has been requested to earmark a dedicated venue on the outskirts of Mysuru for rallies, concerts and other large public events to ease traffic congestion in the city centre.

title: Prime Minister virtually inaugurates three redeveloped railway stations in Andhra Pradesh
content: Rayanapadu, Mangalagiri and Cumbum stations, redeveloped at a cost of ₹59.28 crore under the Amrit Bharat Station Scheme, feature modern passenger amenities and sustainable infrastructure

title: CPI urges Prime Minister Modi to respond to  Wangchuk’s indefinite fast
content: Expressing the party’s solidarity with the environmentalist, CPI leader Eswaraiah says, considering his contributions to water conservation and technology for soldiers, his protest deserves immediate attention

title: DDMA had warned of July 7 debris slip in Wayanad, amicus curiae tells Kerala High Court
content: It says that the District Disaster Management Authority had directed the executing agencies that the excavated earth near the Anakkampoyil–Kalladi–Meppaditwin tube tunnel road project site be removed, to avert a disaster

title: Kerala Knowledge Valley: Government kick-starts process to identify locations, establish linkages
content: The initiative aims to transform Kerala into a global higher education hub, bringing top-tier foreign universities and premier Indian institutions together in a single ecosystem

title: Supreme Court to examine plea for recognition of intersex persons as a distinct class
content: Petitioner seeks nationwide ban on medically unnecessary genital surgeries on intersex children; calls for provision of identity documents and job reservation

title: Ashwin Sanghi to be the first recipient of Bhutan’s JSW prize for literature
content: Sanghi to be honoured at the 15th edition of the Drukyul’s Literature and Arts Festival of Bhutan, with this year’s theme being ‘The Art of Being Human’

title: Ramagiri gold mines set to reopen after 25 years
content: Officials claim improved economic conditions and advances in mining technology have enhanced the prospects of reopening the mines

title: CPI seeks review of Kerala HC order barring political events at Thekkinkadu Maidan
content: Party says Thrissur’s iconic ground has long served as a democratic public space; to hold protest on July 20

title: KSHRC orders probe into alleged denial of basic amenities in Edamalakkudy
content: The commission also ordered a probe into allegations that the Forest department is creating hurdles regarding basic necessities of the tribal population

title: Kanaka Durga temple officials push to complete key works before Shravana Masam
content: Master plan envisages a multi-level parking complex and a second ghat road to accommodate rising pilgrim footfall, improve traffic management and enhance safety at the hill shrine

title: Government committed to delivering welfare schemes to every eligible beneficiary: K.A. Thulasi
content: Minister inaugurates NABARD-backed Athirappilly–Mattathur Tribes project, promises faster implementation of tribal development initiatives

title: Govt. betrayed handloom weavers, alleges YSRCP leader
content: The government has significantly reduced the number of beneficiaries under the assistance schemes, besides weakening APCO, alleges MLC Hanumantha Rao

title: Nagaland Police offer ₹50K reward for leads in July 13 IED blast
content: An Assam Rifles havildar was killed, and four others were critically injured following a blast suspected to have been caused by an improvised explosive device (IED) at Khopanullah under Chümoukedima ‘A’ village

title: Turn challenges into opportunities to succeed in life, Ramesh Chennithala tells students
content: Home Minister inaugurates Thrissur Corporation’s Vidyashree-2026 awards; O.J. Janeesh highlights ‘Operation Toofan’ policing initiative

title: Accor, Sri Bhramara Group to develop ₹250-crore  beachfront hotel at Chirala
content: Spread across a 3.15-acre beachfront site, the proposed hotel features 225–250 guest rooms and suites, multiple restaurants, banquet and conference facilities, a swimming pool, spa, fitness centre and landscaped recreational spaces

title: KUHS warns students against enroling on unrecognised medical, health science courses
content: Vice-Chancellor urges parents and aspirants to verify institutional affiliations and statutory approvals before seeking admission

title: NEET-UG 2026 | A collection of explainers and expert opinions
content: The NEET-UG 2026 ‘paper leak’, and the structural inefficiencies that caused it, explained by The Hindu

title: NEET-UG: Over 2.7 lakh candidates skipped re-test, highest absentee count ever
content: For the re-examination, 19,99,895 candidates appeared compared to 22.05 lakh candidates who had taken the original NEET-UG examination

title: At 15,000 cusecs, Godavari witnesses lowest-ever daily inflow at Polavaram in seven decades, says Water Resources Minister Nimmala Ramanaidu
content: The Minister primarily attributes it to El Niño and negligence of the irrigation system during the YSRCP term; he also advises farmers to go in for short-duration crops and make judicious use of available water

title: 5 dead after train collides with school van in West Bengal’s Murshidabad; 4 children among deceased
content: Railway gateman arrested; CM offers condolences; Opposition raises systematic failure issue; Congress leader Adhir Ranjan Chowdhury visited the accident site

title: India’s first privately developed orbital-class rocket Vikram-1 set for July 18 lift-off
content: Private space launch company Skyroot Aerospace on July 16, 2026 announced that it will attempt to launch Vikram-1 from the space port’s first launch pad at 11.30 a.m..

title: PM Modi inaugurates, lays foundation stone of projects worth ₹5,470 crore in Jalandhar
content: This is his second visit to Jalandhar after he visited the Dera Sachkhand Ballan in February this year

title: Infrastructure gaps loom large as Kerala fishermen prepare to venture into international waters
content: The Union government issues Letters of Authorisation for fishermen to venture into high seas. However, the fishersface challenges such as the high costs of modernising vessels and the shortage of skilled laborers. Guidelines mandate strict adherence to international maritime rules, catch limits, bycatch mitigation strategies, and measures against illegal and unregulated fishing.

title: Anurag Kumar appointed Delhi Police Commissioner
content: 1994-batch IPS officer replaces Satish Golcha ahead of the Monsoon Session of Parliament, with key law and order challenges looming

title: CPI(M) blames civic neglect for diarrhoea outbreak in Vijayawada’s Giripuram
content: Party alleges contaminated drinking water and poor sanitation triggered the outbreak, seeks emergency sanitation measures, safe water supply and inquiry into recurring cases

title: Supreme Court to examine whether persons deleted from voter list in SIR can be denied welfare benefits
content: Congress leader Prasenjit Bose’s petition said only 38,000 of 34 lakh appeals have been heard so far, leaving lakhs excluded from welfare schemes and facing hardship

title: Kerala govt plans multi-layer defence against human-wildlife conflict: Forest Minister
content: Proposed defensive system to comprise AI-based surveillance and early-warning tools, followed by hanging fences and, where necessary, stronger defensive structures

title: Kerala looks to the skies to tackle human-wildlife conflict, but is this feasible?
content: Kerala Forest Department has proposed using Indian Air Force helicopters to relocate captured Schedule I animals, including tigers and leopards, from conflict hotspots to secure forest habitats. However, the move has sparked debate over its ecological and economic feasibility

title: Three-tier strategy proposed to address rising human-wildlife conflict
content: The plan focuses on containment, mitigation, and compensation to enhance safety for farmers near forests and provide timely relief to affected families. The Forest department will utilize AI and sensor-based systems to study wildlife behaviour and promote coexistence.

title: Asaram's plea for interim bail: Don't want any untoward thing to happen, says Supreme Court
content: The self-styled godman has filed an application seeking interim bail on health grounds; the Bench posted the matter for hearing on July 21.

title: Rajasthan High Court confirms godman Asaram’s life sentence, drops gangrape charge
content: The court directed Asaram to surrender in view of the conviction; he is currently out on temporary bail, which was extended by seven days on May 25

title: PM Modi announced Ram Temple trust in Parliament, must break silence on floor of House: Congress
content: The Trust was set up by the Prime Minister and it comprises people appointed by him, Jairam Ramesh said, adding the Trust's activities, its functions, its terms of reference were given by Mr. Modi

title: Ram temple donation embezzlement: SIT to submit interim report on Monday
content: Sources further said the SIT may seek more time from the Uttar Pradesh government to finalise its investigation into the alleged financial irregularities related to donations received by the Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust.

title: Telangana launches ‘New Employees Health Scheme’ for 17.88 lakh govt employees, pensioners and their family members
content: The process of claiming insurance, from pre-authorisation to claims settlement, is digitised, says Health Minister C Damodar Raja Narasimha

title: Sitaphalmandi-Lallaguda 3 km rail chord line gives trains a faster route, eases pressure on Secunderabad station
content: The Railways completed key infrastructure works on the stretch, including five minor bridges, a major road-over-bridge at the Alugadda Bhavi junction between Rail Nilayam and Mettuguda Road

title: India weathered West Asia crisis through strategic decision-making, diplomacy: PM Modi
content: India avoided fuel rationing, LPG disruptions, price surges, with domestic refineries upping their LPG production and public sector OMCs bearing the losses, says PM; promises further expansion of refinery capacity

title: In PM Modi's Punjab Speech, References To Drugs And Tahir Hussain
content: He claimed the Centre had sent large sums of money to Punjab over the years, but the funds were being misused.

title: Kuwait Says Army Personnel Injured In Iranian Drone Attack
content: The army's chief of staff "visited several wounded members of the Kuwaiti land forces", it said in a press statement.

title: Iran Strikes US Airbase In Qatar "To Punish The Aggressor"
content: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps said Friday that they had targeted US radar systems and military aircraft in Qatar.

title: 'No Hatred': Australia Vows Crackdown On Racism Against Indian Truckers
content: Asked about a rise in racial slurs, verbal harassment and even death threats delivered over CB radio to Indian-origin drivers, Burke acknowledged the trend as a "grim reality" and laid out a two-part response: what the government is doing and what it is saying, insisting both matter equally.

title: On Camera, Elderly Man Slapped By Auto Driver After Argument In Bengaluru
content: An elderly man was slapped by an autorickshaw driver following a heated argument on a Bengaluru road, with the entire incident captured on a nearby car's dashboard camera.

title: Congress To Join Omar Abdullah's Protest For Jammu And Kashmir's Statehood
content: Tariq Hamid Karra, the J&K Congress President, said the party's central leadership will also join the protests on July 20.

title: Hotter Nights Stealing Sleep Across World, India Among Hardest Hit
content: Cities across the Middle East recorded the highest heat-related sleep losses, while parts of South Asia, Southeast Asia and West Africa also showed significant impacts.

title: Skyroot's Vikram-1 To Carry Micro-Sculptures Of Indian Icons Into Space
content: If the mission succeeds, Vikram-1 could become the first Indian rocket to carry micro art into space, creating a unique fusion of art, science and space exploration.

title: "Rs 7 Lakh Price Set For Lost Life": Mumbai Mayor On Tree Collapse Report
content: The Brihanmumbai Municipal Corporation (BMC) held the contractors accountable and gave a clean-chit to its own officials.

title: In Flood-Battered Arunachal, Elephants Become The Only Lifeline
content: Shattered by nature, villagers are relying on elephants to transport essential supplies to remote villages in the flood-ravaged Nari-Koyu constituency of Arunachal Pradesh's Lower Siang district.

title: Bangladesh Protesters Seek Death For Kolkata MLA Over Airport Mosque Row
content: A protest rally was organised in Bangladesh by local residents and several Muslim organisations following reports of restrictions on Friday prayers at the Bankra Mosque, also known as the Gouripur Jama Masjid, located within the Kolkata Airport premises.

title: Teen Shooter Disappears After Going Out For Groceries. Last Seen At Station
content: Her family reported her missing at the local police station after searches among friends, shooting associates, and relatives yielded no information.

title: Andy Burnham To Take Oath As Britain's Prime Minister Next Week
content: Burnham brings a more relaxed style of leadership than the rather stern Starmer, and is regarded as one of the Labour Party's best communicators.

title: Bharat Tex 2026 Brings Rs 14,300-Crore Investment Commitments
content: More than 1.15 lakh people visited the third edition of Bharat Tex as the four-day global textile trade fair drew to a close on Friday.

title: Rs 40 Lakh Compensation For Family Of Child Killed In 2020 Delhi Accident
content: Presiding Officer Sunil Kumar was hearing a claim petition filed by the parents of the child and said that the accident occurred due to the rash and negligent driving of the car.

title: Delhi Police Bust Fake Currency Racket, Kingpin Among 3 Arrested
content: The racket came to light after a shopkeeper in Bhalswa Dairy detected counterfeit currency while accepting payment from a customer, the police said.

title: 1 Highway. 4 States. Millions Of Lives: PM Modi Opens Delhi-Katra Expressway
content: The newly inaugurated stretch is part of the 667-km Delhi-Amritsar-Katra Expressway, being developed at an estimated cost of around Rs 38,905 crore.

title: PM Modi Launches Rail, Road Projects Worth Rs 5,470 Crore In Punjab
content: The projects are aimed at strengthening connectivity, improving passenger convenience and accelerating economic development in the region.

title: Key Accused Arrested In Assam Madrassa Sexual Abuse Case
content: The private girls' madrassa in Assam's Bongaigaon district was sealed by the district administration on Wednesday, following allegations that the minor girl students were sexually abused.

title: G.D.N Release Date: R Madhavan-led biopic to hit theatres on August 7; Netflix run to follow
content: The TOI Entertainment Desk is a dynamic and dedicated team of journalists, working tirelessly to bring the pulse of the entertainment world straight to the readers of The Times of India. No red carpet goes unrolled, no stage goes dark - our team spans the globe, bringing you the latest scoops and insider insights from Bollywood to Hollywood, and every entertainment hotspot in between. We don't just report; we tell tales of stardom and stories untold. Whether it's the rise of a new sensation or the seasoned journey of an industry veteran, the TOI Entertainment Desk is your front-row seat to the fascinating narratives that shape the entertainment landscape. Beyond the breaking news, we present a celebration of culture. We explore the intersections of entertainment with society, politics, and everyday life. Read More

title: 'No proof, no facts': Former envoy Sanjay Verma on Nijjar case indictment
content: former Indian ambassador to canada defends india's position on the case . he says allegations linking India to the killing were "baseless" and unsupported by evidence . new Delhi has consistently warned Canada about Khalistani extremist activities .

title: Pirates allegedly hijack chemical tanker Asana in Gulf of Aden off Yemen
content: chemical tanker ship Asana believed to have been hijacked by armed assailants . incident occurred as tanker was transiting east off the southern coast of Yemen . ship did not have a confirmed flag .

content: Abhishek Mishra is a Digital Content Producer at The Times of India, working with the news desk. He writes with a keen eye on foreign policy, defence developments, and the shifting currents of geopolitics-and yes, Indian politics always finds a way onto his radar too. Read More

title: Consumer panel fines SpiceJet for ‘deploying dark patterns on its booking platform’
content: the airline has been directed to permanently discontinue default-consent practices . the violations include: 'automatic enrolment into SpiceClub. Pre-selected consent for promotional communications. Deceptive interface design influencing consumer decisions'

title: 'Who is D nexT': Iran billboard targets Trump after Graham's death
content: the billboard features the name of recently deceased US Senator Lindsey Graham . it features what appears to be a hidden reference to assasination threats for president . conservative voices in Iran have portrayed Graham's death as divine punishment .

title: Skyroot’s rocket Vikram-1 will carry to space a special message from PM Modi
content: skyroot's Vikram-1 will fly to space at 11.30am on saturday . it will carry a message for the universe from Prime Minister Narendra Modi . the handwritten card has PM Modi’s signature and has the date 26-06-26 written in his writing .

title: Andy Burnham set to be new UK PM, will take over from Keir Starmer on Monday
content: he was the only contender in the leadership contest to replace departing Prime Minister Keir Starmer . he secured nominations from 379 of the 403 Labour lawmakers in the house of commons . the former mayor of Greater Manchester has been prime minister-in-waiting for weeks .

title: 'Whoever kills Trump ...': Iran-backed Iraqi militia puts $10m bounty on US prez
content: Kanchan Yadav is a writer at The Times of India, where she covers business, politics, international affairs and social issues. Her reporting also focuses on policy, governance and economic developments, with a close lens on how decisions in corridors of power affect everyday lives and community well-being. Read More

title: 'Individual opinion': India rejects ex-Japan minister's claims on bullet train delays
content: Trisha Mahajan is a journalist with The Times of India digital, with keen interest in covering business and corporate affairs. She also reports on key national and world developments and global news trends. A keen reader with a strong interest in storytelling that adds context, clarity, and depth to complex issues. Read More

title: 'Absurd, bogus': US VP JD Vance rejects report Kushner, Witkoff profiting from Iran talks
content: Vance made the remarks in response to a report that claimed Iran had accused Kushner and Witkoff of exploiting the negotiations for financial gain . he said he never received an alleged warning from Tehran about the two senior members of president Donald Trump's team .
